name: caen-felib

metadata.name: caen-felib
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU Lesser General Public License v3 or later (LGPLv3+)
metadata.summary: Official Python binding for the CAEN FELib.

documentation:
# caen-felib
The official Python binding for the CAEN FELib.

## Install
You need to install both the latest version of CAEN FELib and an implementation like the CAEN Dig2 from [the CAEN website](https://www.caen.it/products/caen-felib-library/).

Then, install this module and have fun:

    pip install caen-felib

## Examples
Few examples may be found on the [`examples`](https://github.com/caenspa/py-caen-felib/tree/master/examples) folder of the project repository.

## Copyright notice
Copyright © 2024 CAEN SpA

License** as published by the Free Software Foundation; either

The caen-felib module is distributed in the hope that it will be useful,
but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of
MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE. See the GNU
Lesser General Public License for more details.

You should have received a copy of the GNU Lesser General Public License along
with the caen-felib module; if not, see https://www.gnu.org/licenses/.

The license applies to the caen-felib module source as a whole, though
individual source files can have a different license which is required to be
compatible with the GNU Lesser General Public Library, version 3.

## Documentation
Python API is described on the CAEN FELib library documentation, starting from CAEN FELib v1.2.3.

## References
Requirements and documentation can be found at https://www.caen.it/products/caen-felib-library/.

## Support
For technical support, go to https://www.caen.it/mycaen/support/ (login and
MyCAEN+ account required).

documentation: Identifies the first event after a dead time.

documentation: Identifies a trigger time stamp roll-over that occurred before this event.

documentation: Identifies a trigger time stamp reset forced from external signals in S-IN (GPI for Desktop).

class_variables.name: caen_felib.dig1_types.DppFlags.EVTFAKE
documentation: Identifies a fake event (which does not correspond to any physical event).

class_variables.name: caen_felib.dig1_types.DppFlags.MEMFULL
documentation: Reading memory full.

class_variables.name: caen_felib.dig1_types.DppFlags.TRGLOST
documentation: Identifies the first event after a trigger lost.

class_variables.name: caen_felib.dig1_types.DppFlags.NTRGLOST
documentation: Every N lost events this flag is high (see board documentation to set N).

class_variables.name: caen_felib.dig1_types.DppFlags.OVERRNG
documentation: Energy overranged.

class_variables.name: caen_felib.dig1_types.DppFlags.F1024TRG
documentation: 1024 triggers counted (every 1024 counted events this flag is high).

class_variables.name: caen_felib.dig1_types.DppFlags.LOSTEVT
documentation: Identifies the first event after when one or more events is lost due to a memory board FULL. The memory can be FULL due to a write event.

class_variables.name: caen_felib.dig1_types.DppFlags.INPUTSAT
documentation: Identifies an event saturating the input dynamics (clipping).

class_variables.name: caen_felib.dig1_types.DppFlags.NTRGTOT
documentation: Every N total events this flag is high (see board documentation to set N).

class_variables.name: caen_felib.dig1_types.DppFlags.OLDSORT
documentation: Identifies an event not sorted but sent for waveform.

class_variables.name: caen_felib.dig1_types.DppFlags.EOR
documentation: Identifies a fake event occurring at the end of run.

class_variables.name: caen_felib.dig1_types.DppFlags.FINETT
documentation: Identifies an event with fine time stamp.

class_variables.name: caen_felib.dig1_types.DppFlags.PILEUP
documentation: Identifies a pile up event.

class_variables.name: caen_felib.dig1_types.DppFlags.TIMEVALUE
documentation: Identifies a fake event occurring on a time stamp roll-over.

class_variables.name: caen_felib.dig1_types.DppFlags.ENERGY_SKIM
documentation: Energy skimming.

class_variables.name: caen_felib.dig1_types.DppFlags.SATREJ
documentation: Identifies an event occurred when detector was inibited due to saturation.

class_variables.name: caen_felib.dig1_types.DppFlags.PLLLOCKLOSS
documentation: Identifies a fake event sent to report a PLL lock loss.

class_variables.name: caen_felib.dig1_types.DppFlags.OVERTEMP
documentation: Identifies a fake event sent to report an over temperature condition.

class_variables.name: caen_felib.dig1_types.DppFlags.SHUTDOWN
documentation: Identifies a fake event sent to report an ADC shutdown.

class_variables.name: caen_felib.dig1_types.DppFlags.MEMORYSORT
documentation: Identifies a fake event sent to report a memory overload during data sorting.

class_variables.name: caen_felib.dig1_types.DppFlags.MCS
documentation: Identifies a MCS event

class_variables.name: caen_felib.dig1_types.DppFlags.STOPCOND
documentation: Identifies the first event after a stop condition has been met.
Flags on Dig1 DPP events

modules.name: caen_felib._cache
documentation: Cache utilities

functions.name: caen_felib._cache.cached
documentation:
LRU cache decorator that keeps a weak reference to self.

To be used as decorator on methods that are known to return always
the same value. This can improve the performances of some methods by
a factor > 1000.
This wrapper using weak references is required: functools.lru_cache
holds a reference to all arguments: using directly on the methods it
would hold a reference to self, introducing subdle memory leaks.

@sa https://stackoverflow.com/a/68052994/3287591
Typing support for decorators comes with Python 3.10.
Omitted because very verbose.

functions.name: caen_felib._cache.clear
documentation:
LRU cache decorator that clear cache.

To be used as decorator on methods that are known to invalidate the
cache.

classes.name: caen_felib._cache.Manager

methods.name: caen_felib._cache.Manager.clear_all
documentation: Invoke `cache_clear` on all functions in the list

A simple list of functions returned by `@lru_cache` decorator.

To be used with the optional parameter @p cache_manager of
lru_cache_method(), that will store a reference to the cached
function inside this list. This is a typing-safe way to call
`cache_clear` and `cache_info` of the internal cached functions,
even if not exposed directly by the inner function returned by
lru_cache_method().

methods.name: caen_felib.device.Node.close
documentation:
Binding of CAEN_FELib_Close()

This will also clear class cache to remove all references to
child nodes. It will impact also nodes of other digitizer, but
is required to allow garbage collection of unused nodes.

@exception                                      error.Error in case of error

caen_felib.device.Node.set_read_data_format
documentation:
Binding of CAEN_FELib_SetReadDataFormat()

In addition to what happens in C library, it also allocate data.
Size of fields with `dim > 0` must be specified by a `"shape"`
entry in the field description, that is a vector passed to the
`shape` argument of `np.empty` constructor. On fields with
`dim == 0` the shape can be omitted, and is set to `[]` by
default. Fields can be accessed on data attribute of this class,
that is a list of Data inizialized with the field descriptions,
in the same order of @p format.

Example:
```
nch = dig.par.numch.value
reclen = dig.par.recordlengths.value
format = [
    {
        'name': 'WAVEFORM',
        'type': 'U16',
        'dim': 2,
        'shape': [nch, reclen],
    },
]
data = ep_node.set_read_data_format(format)
print(data[0])
```

@param[in] fmt                          JSON representation of the format, in compliance with the endpoint "format" property (a list of dictionaries)
@return                                         Tuple of Data with allocated buffers of specified dim and shape, to be passed as second argument of read_data()
@exception                                      error.Error in case of error
